headline: Suddenly Hawaiʻi Lawmakers Aren’t Talking Much About Those Big Tax Cuts
summary: Hawaii officials face political pressure to reverse promised tax breaks in an election year, indicating state budget constraints that may require revenue adjustments and fiscal policy changes.

headline: Amid ICE enforcement fears, Ramadan binds local Muslims in solidarity, support
summary: Federal immigration enforcement, specifically ICE, has targeted refugee and asylum seeker communities in Vermont, including the detention of a Somalian mosque member on New Year's Day. This enforcement has created heightened fear among attendees during Ramadan. The Islamic Society of Vermont is working to reassure community members of their safety. In response, community members organized mutual aid and provided courtroom support for the detained individual upon his release on bail.

headline: ‘We can only go up from here’: Hope and apathy in Wilkinson County schools
summary: The Mississippi Board of Education voted in January to take over the Wilkinson County School District due to chronically low math and reading scores since 2018-19 and dysfunctional leadership. State investigations revealed school board members violated purchasing laws, approved payments without proper review, and interfered in daily operations, resulting in termination of their service. The district faces severe teacher vacancies in state-tested subjects, with substitute teachers filling most algebra and English II courses. A state investigation into testing irregularities remains open, and the department invalidated high school test scores, dropping the district's accountability grade. The takeover reflects systemic challenges including a 50% student population decline over six years and 30% community poverty.
